title: seeing different manifest versions for an image built on centos 8 and fedora 33
body:
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm seeing podman use different manifest types when I build on Fedora 33 versus Centos 8:

On Fedora I get the folllowing when I run 'podman inspect':
"ManifestType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
"io.buildah.version": "1.16.1"

On Centos 8 I get the following when I run 'podman inspect':
 "ManifestType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
 "io.buildah.version": "1.16.1",

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run podman inspect on a built container on Fedora 32/32

2. run podman inspect on a built container image on Centos 8


**Describe the results you received:**

the manifest versions are different

**Describe the results you expected:**
the manifest versions would be the same

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
on fedora 33:
```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Oct  7 11:21:20 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

on Centos 8:

```
[jeffmc@snoot ~]$ podman version
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Fri Oct 30 18:23:30 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64```

```
podman-2.1.1-12.fc33.x86_64
```
**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
VM running under KVM on a Fedora physical host.

comments.body: I did some more testing of this today, I can't reproduce it sadly.  What I do notice is that I have to specify --format=docker to get either of these to build a v2 manifest which I guess is expected?   I still see issues when I push these to quay.io, in that if I pull down the images from quay.io, I get back a v1 manifest image.  But I think that is being tracked in another issue.
Ok I am going to close, reopen if it happens again.
